title: Ternary Expressions Cause Assertion Failure
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.50+7 (git sha1 38f858374, clang++ 14.0.0-1ubuntu1.1 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

I encountered an assertion failure in Yosys while processing a Verilog design. I used bugpoint to reduce the code to a minimal test case.

[rtl.v.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19412948/rtl.v.txt)

This failure can be reproduced with the following script:
```
read_verilog 3510.v
prep
splitcells
```
Minimisation Code:
```
# Generated by Yosys 0.50+7 (git sha1 38f858374, clang++ 14.0.0-1ubuntu1.1 -fPIC -O3)
autoidx 3064
attribute \src "rtl.v:874.1-1178.10"
module \module122
  attribute $bugpoint 1
  wire output 1 $auto$bugpoint.cc:258:simplify_something$3056
  attribute \src "rtl.v:994.42-996.47"
  cell $sshl $sshl$rtl.v:994$755
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    parameter \A_WIDTH 1
    parameter \B_SIGNED 0
    parameter \B_WIDTH 35
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 1
    connect \A 1'x
    connect \B 35'x
    connect \Y $auto$bugpoint.cc:258:simplify_something$3056
  end
end
```

### Expected Behavior

The command should not cause an assert failure.

### Actual Behavior

```
3. Executing SPLITCELLS pass (splitting up multi-bit cells).
Splitting $dff cell top/$procdff$38 into 2 slices:
  slice 1: $procdff$38[1:0] => \reg127 [1:0]
  slice 2: $procdff$38[2] => \reg127 [2]
Splitting $xnor cell top/$xnor$rtl.v:25$28 into 2 slices:
  slice 1: $xnor$rtl.v:25$28[1:0] => $0\reg127[3:0] [1:0]
ERROR: Assert `length >= 0' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:4880.
```
code_blocks:
# Generated by Yosys 0.50+7 (git sha1 38f858374, clang++ 14.0.0-1ubuntu1.1 -fPIC -O3)
autoidx 3064
attribute \src "rtl.v:874.1-1178.10"
module \module122
  attribute $bugpoint 1
  wire output 1 $auto$bugpoint.cc:258:simplify_something$3056
  attribute \src "rtl.v:994.42-996.47"
  cell $sshl $sshl$rtl.v:994$755
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    parameter \A_WIDTH 1
    parameter \B_SIGNED 0
    parameter \B_WIDTH 35
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 1
    connect \A 1'x
    connect \B 35'x
    connect \Y $auto$bugpoint.cc:258:simplify_something$3056
  end
end

3. Executing SPLITCELLS pass (splitting up multi-bit cells).
Splitting $dff cell top/$procdff$38 into 2 slices:
  slice 1: $procdff$38[1:0] => \reg127 [1:0]
  slice 2: $procdff$38[2] => \reg127 [2]
Splitting $xnor cell top/$xnor$rtl.v:25$28 into 2 slices:
  slice 1: $xnor$rtl.v:25$28[1:0] => $0\reg127[3:0] [1:0]
ERROR: Assert `length >= 0' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:4880.
